summary: Large decompilation/matching PR for Kirby copied neutral-special code in src/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs.c. It replaced many placeholder asm stubs with C implementations, especially the ftKb_PrSpecialN* animation/IASA/physics/collision path, plus smaller Ns and Dk special-N matches. The decomp.dev bot reported 42 new matches and +19284 matched bytes, bringing matched code to 60.38% (+0.50%). Header and struct changes supported the matches, notably converting three scalar Kirby fighter-vars fields x8C/x90/x94 into a Vec3 x8C used as stored JObj scale.

key_files.role: Main matching work: added 1349 lines and removed many placeholder markers for NsSpecial, DkSpecial, and especially PrSpecialN handlers, helpers, callbacks, physics, collision, effects, scale/rotation code.
Changed ftKb_FighterVars offsets 0x8C/0x90/0x94 from three floats into Vec3 x8C, enabling JObj scale save/restore code to typecheck and match.
Updated initialization of fp->fv.kb.x8C from scalar assignments to x/y/z Vec3 component assignments.
Updated ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C prototype to take a bool arg1.
Kept Doxygen prototype for ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C in sync with the new bool parameter.

what_changed: Matched 42 items in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs according to the decomp.dev bot; top reported matches included ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Coll, ftKb_PrSpecialAirN_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialN1_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialN1_Coll, ftKb_PrSpecialNTurn_Phys, ftKb_PrSpecialNTurn_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialAirNEndR_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialNEnd_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialN_Anim, ftKb_PrSpecialNHit_Anim, ftKb_SpecialNPr_801010D4, and fn_80100E0C.
Implemented ftKb_NsSpecialAirNStart_Anim and ftKb_NsSpecialAirNHold_Anim and factored shared flash-item spawning into ftKb_NsSpecialNStart_Anim_inline using lb_8000B1CC and it_802AA8C0.

Implemented Dk copied special-N entry helpers ftKb_SpecialNDk_800FF8EC and ftKb_SpecialNDk_800FFA10, including full-charge branch selection, command/move-var reset, callbacks, hitlag effect pause/resume callbacks, and ftAnim_8006EBA4.

Implemented a large PrSpecialN/Rollout-like flow: setup helpers ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010131C, damage/hitbox helper ftKb_SpecialNPr_80100F94, end-state helper ftKb_SpecialNPr_801010D4, roll-sound/angle helper ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C, animation handlers, IASA handlers, physics handlers, ground/air collision handlers, hit collision, wall-bounce effect handling, and callback reset helpers.
Added local static inline 'fake HSD_JObj helpers' for get/set scale, dirty-matrix checks, and Y rotation, including __assert call shapes and JOBJ flag checks.

Added helper routines for repeated PrSpecialN behavior: ftKb_PrScaleAnimStep, ftKb_AirScaleAnimStep, ftKb_PrRestoreScale, ftKb_PrSetCallbacks, ftKb_PrNormalizeAndSetRollAngle, ftKb_PrHitCapsuleToggle, ftKb_PrSetFacingDir, and ftKb_PrWallBounceEffect.
Updated includes needed by the new matches, including camera, landing, ft_0877, ftcliffcommon, mpLib, and MSL trig support.
Reworked existing air Ns physics to call ftKb_NsSpecialAirNStart_Phys from hold/end variants with PAD_STACK, replacing earlier fake-match comma/regalloc comments.
Updated ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C declaration in both header and dox from one parameter to two parameters: Fighter_GObj* gobj and bool arg1.
smart_moves: A struct-layout insight converted fp->fv.kb.x8C/x90/x94 into Vec3 x8C, which made scale save/restore code natural and removed awkward scalar treatment at adjacent offsets.
Repeated callback assignments were centralized in ftKb_PrSetCallbacks, preserving death/take-damage/deal-damage/x21F8 callback behavior while keeping C code readable.

Scale and rotation behavior was isolated into inline helpers that mimic HSD_JObj operations, including assert strings and matrix-dirty flag behavior, rather than scattering raw field writes everywhere.
Ground and air PrSpecialN variants reused paired helper logic where possible but still preserved distinct motion IDs, motion flags, velocities, and fall/freefall behavior.

The match uses explicit small stack pads, local Vec3 temporaries, static motion-flag constants, and carefully shaped inline helpers to satisfy MWCC/regalloc constraints without leaving placeholder stubs.

Collision handling preserves specific environment-bit tests for left/right wall cases, effect spawning, camera shake, SFX, cliff checks, and landing/freefall transitions instead of simplifying gameplay-visible behavior.
decomp_lessons: Adjacent unknown floats at stable offsets may actually be a vector; confirming usage through JObj scale code can unlock cleaner and more matchable struct definitions.
For decomp matching, local inline wrappers around library-like behavior can be useful when the exact call/assert/dirty-flag shape matters more than API cleanliness.
When multiple action states share identical physics, small wrapper functions plus PAD_STACK can match better than duplicating large bodies or using fake comma-operator regalloc tricks.
Motion-state code often needs exact bitmask constants, callback restoration, and current-frame/anim-rate handling; helper extraction is safe only when the compiler inlines into the expected shape.
Kirby copied-special code crosses fighter, item, effect, camera, cliff, and stage-collision systems, so includes and prototypes often need to be fixed along with the C body.
Keep header/dox prototypes synchronized immediately when a matched function reveals an extra parameter, as happened with ftKb_SpecialNPr_8010140C(gobj, bool arg1).
naming_conventions: Kirby symbols use the ftKb_ prefix, with copied-special groups encoded in names such as NsSpecial, DkSpecial, and PrSpecialN.

Motion state symbols follow ftKb_MS_* names with explicit ground/air and phase suffixes, such as ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNLoop, ftKb_MS_PrSpecialAirNLoop, ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNFull, ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNTurn, and ftKb_MS_PrSpecialNHit.
Per-character data attributes remain in the specialn_pr_* and specialn_ns_* style; unknowns are still named conservatively, e.g. specialn_pr_unk, specialn_pr_unk10, x0, x2C, x34.
New local helpers use descriptive ftKb_Pr* names for repeated behavior: ftKb_PrSetCallbacks, ftKb_PrRestoreScale, ftKb_PrScaleAnimStep, ftKb_PrNormalizeAndSetRollAngle, ftKb_PrWallBounceEffect.
The PR did not rename many unknown move variables; it preserved existing xNN field names where semantic evidence was weak.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Used PAD_STACK with explicit byte sizes and occasional u8 pad arrays to shape stack layout.
Used static inline helper functions to force inlining for JObj scale/rotation helpers and repeated PrSpecialN behaviors.
Used static u32 const motion-flag bitmasks instead of ad hoc numeric literals in several matched handlers.
Preserved exact float/math forms such as M_PI, M_PI_2, deg_to_rad, 0.017453292f, ABS, SIGNF, atan2f, and small constants like 0.0001f and -0.05f.
Replaced previous fake comma-operator regalloc comments in Ns air physics with explicit locals and PAD_STACK.
Matched library-like JObj behavior by reproducing __assert calls and JOBJ_USER_DEF_MTX, JOBJ_MTX_DIRTY, JOBJ_MTX_INDEP_SRT, and JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION flag checks.
Kept ground/air collision branches separate enough to preserve calls to ft_80082708, ft_80081D0C, ft_80082888, ft_8008239C, ft_800824A0, ftCliffCommon_80081298, and ftCliffCommon_80081370.
review_feedback: No human review comments or PR body text were present in the slice.
The decomp.dev bot reported matched code 60.38% (+0.50%, +19284 bytes), 42 new matches in ftKb_SpecialNNs, and one .sdata2 improvement from 42.50% to 75.93%.
follow_up_queries: Look for later PRs that replace the local fake HSD_JObj helpers in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c with shared baselib helpers or better-known inline patterns.
Search for follow-up renames of ftKb_FighterVars.x8C and mv.pr.specialn x-fields after the Vec3 scale discovery.
Check whether the remaining .sdata2 in main/melee/ft/chara/ftKirby/ftKb_SpecialNNs was fully matched after this PR.
Compare PrSpecialN/Rollout helper patterns in ftKb_SpecialNNs.c with native ftPr SpecialN code for reusable naming and behavior evidence.
Search for other Kirby copied-special files where adjacent float fields should become Vec3 or where callback setup can be centralized similarly.
